subtitle: Data-Driven Agile Delivery & Flow Optimization
hero_sub: 15+ years leading Agile delivery across complex, cross-functional software initiatives — using velocity, WIP, cycle time, throughput, and cumulative flow data to drive sprint planning, forecasting, and continuous improvement, not just facilitate ceremonies.

the_case.need: 8+ years Scrum Master and/or Project Management experience
the_case.answer: 15+ years of SM/delivery leadership across Eptura, Crocs, Understood.org, A&E, DigitasLBI/SunTrust, and more.
the_case.detail: Comfortably exceeds the stated threshold — Scrum Master and Agile delivery leadership has been the consistent throughline of my career since 2014.

the_case.need: 5+ years supporting Agile software development teams; deep SDLC/Scrum/Kanban knowledge
the_case.answer: Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid Agile facilitation across every engagement since 2014, including SAFe/PI-aligned programs at Eptura and DigitasLBI/SunTrust.
the_case.detail: Every engagement has required a different blend of Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid Agile depending on the team and the work — I adapt the framework to the team, not the other way around.

the_case.need: Strong experience using Agile metrics and reporting to guide sprint planning, forecasting, performance improvement
the_case.answer: At Eptura, built and maintained Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, WIP, and cumulative flow across a ~10-team program, driving a 35%+ efficiency gain and 20% carryover reduction.
the_case.detail: The dashboards weren't a reporting exercise — they were how we forecast delivery risk before it became a missed sprint commitment. That's the exact distinction this posting draws between facilitating ceremonies and driving decisions with data.

the_case.answer: Eptura (~10 distributed teams, India/California), Understood.org (12+ countries), A&E (~10 concurrent workstreams).
the_case.detail: Cross-functional, multi-continent coordination has been the default operating condition, not a stretch assignment.

the_case.answer: Certified Scrum Master (CSM), Scrum Alliance — current.
the_case.detail: Exact match on the required certification.

the_case.need: Healthcare, life sciences, or regulated industry experience (preferred)
the_case.answer: SOX-compliant delivery at Crocs (public retailer) and DigitasLBI/SunTrust (financial services) — not healthcare-specific, but genuine regulated-industry audit/change-control discipline.
the_case.detail: Not healthcare specifically, but the audit discipline and change-control rigor regulated industries require transfers directly from these engagements.

the_stack.headline: The Agile metrics toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools used daily to turn ceremonies into decisions.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Administration-level Jira fluency — dashboards, velocity, WIP, cumulative flow, AI-assisted reporting
nodes.pm_role: Built and maintained flow-metrics dashboards at Eptura across a ~10-team program. Embedded Jira AI at Crocs for a ~30% administrative overhead reduction.

nodes.label: Confluence & Smartsheet
nodes.pm_role: Used across every engagement for executive reporting and stakeholder documentation.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps
nodes.desc: Cloud delivery coordination for enterprise SaaS and retail environments
nodes.pm_role: Coordinated release readiness on Azure/Kubernetes infrastructure at Eptura, Crocs, and Understood.org.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT, Jira AI, Microsoft Copilot
nodes.pm_role: Daily practitioner since 2022. Embedded AI tools directly into ceremonies at Crocs to cut administrative overhead ~30%.

nodes.label: GitHub Actions
nodes.pm_role: Used across recent projects for build-and-deploy automation.

when_things_break.scenario: A program is heading toward a missed sprint commitment, but no one sees it coming until it's too late
response: At Eptura, I built Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, WIP, and cumulative flow across a ~10-team program
Used that data to forecast delivery risk before it became a missed commitment
Flagged risk at the dashboard level, not at the sprint review when it's too late to act
Result: 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover

when_things_break.scenario: A data pipeline or delivery workflow has a hidden bottleneck no one has diagnosed
response: At a stealth-mode AI startup, ran root cause analysis on a throughput bottleneck across a data processing pipeline
Identified the actual constraint rather than treating symptoms
Re-sequenced the workflow around the real bottleneck
Result: throughput improved ~30%, delivery predictability strengthened

when_things_break.scenario: Cross-environment release dependencies threaten a go-live and leadership has no visibility into readiness
response: At Crocs, built 'Release Radar,' a Jira-based flow-visibility framework
Gave leadership real-time readiness data across every environment
Surfaced dependency risk before it could derail the go-live
Result: protected an on-time go-live with clean post-launch stabilization

how_i_run_a_room: Every ceremony generates data — velocity, WIP, cycle time, cumulative flow — and that data drives the next planning decision, not just the retro discussion
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks named before they become issues
Executive dashboards translate sprint-level data into program-level forecasting language leadership can act on
Root cause analysis on delivery misses — not just 'what happened' but 'what pattern will happen again if we don't fix the underlying cause'
Backlog readiness and WIP discipline enforced consistently across Scrum and Kanban teams alike
Go/no-go criteria and change-control gates defined before the sprint, not after it fails

points: Built and maintained Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, WIP, and cumulative flow
Facilitated Scrum-of-Scrums across ~10 distributed teams spanning India and California
Drove 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover
Produced executive dashboards for senior leadership decision-making
Built 'Release Radar,' a Jira-based flow-visibility tracking framework
Designed SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls in sprint workflow
Embedded Jira AI and Microsoft Copilot — ~30% administrative overhead reduction
Facilitated ceremonies across Engineering, QA, Change Management, and Business
Facilitated Agile ceremonies for a distributed team across 12+ countries
Steered a Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform to on-time launch
Primary point of contact for the VP of Engineering
Delivered through QA/UAT with no critical release regressions
Scrum Master within a SAFe enterprise program for a financial-services client

before: Ceremonies happen but don't generate real decisions
Leadership finds out about delivery risk after it's already a problem
The same bottleneck recurs because no one ran root cause analysis
after: Dashboards that forecast risk before it becomes a miss
Ceremonies that produce real decisions, not just status updates
Leadership sees risk early enough to act on it
Root cause analysis prevents the same bottleneck from recurring

the_ramp.deliverable: Review current sprint/program health data (velocity, WIP, cumulative flow if available), shadow a ceremony for each team supported, and read existing backlog and RAID documentation.
Own ceremony facilitation for all supported teams; begin standing up or improving existing flow-metrics dashboards.
Own full reporting cadence for all supported teams; identify 2-3 process improvements from initial root cause analysis of current delivery patterns.
